seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Amazon Bedrock Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Amazon Bedrock Agents enable structured data extraction from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM calls, action groups, and knowledge bases for enterprise-scale deployments. Integrating Vex provides runtime verification to ensure schema compliance, field accuracy, and completeness amid common agent failures like malformed Lambda responses and session state loss. Guardrails are critical to mitigate risks in production data extraction pipelines.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Amazon Bedrock Agents
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with document ingestion into a Bedrock Knowledge Base, where RAG retrieves relevant chunks for the Agent. The Agent uses schema-guided prompts and action groups (Lambda functions) to invoke foundation models like Claude for structured extraction. Guardrails pre- and post-process inputs/outputs for PII and policy compliance.

Multi-turn conversations maintain session state via Bedrock's memory, with Lambda actions handling complex parsing or validation. Outputs are verified for schema adherence before storage in DynamoDB or S3. This setup scales for high-volume document processing but risks hallucinations or incomplete extractions without verification.

Vex wraps agent invocations to verify extracted JSON against predefined schemas, catching issues like missing fields or type mismatches post-inference.

risks.name: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
risks.description: Lambda functions in action groups often return non-JSON or incomplete responses, breaking downstream parsing.
risks.mitigation: Wrap invoke_agent() with Vex.verify() using JSON schema validation on action outputs.

risks.name: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
risks.description: Poor chunking strategies cause incomplete context, leading to missing fields in extractions.
risks.mitigation: Verify extraction completeness against expected field sets using Vex field presence checks.

risks.name: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
risks.description: Bedrock guardrails miss business-specific rules like required field formats or value ranges.
risks.mitigation: Layer Vex custom assertions for domain policies beyond Bedrock's generic filters.

risks.name: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
risks.description: Multi-turn extractions lose context, causing inconsistent field values across interactions.
risks.mitigation: Use Vex session tracking to validate field consistency across agent turns.

implementation.title: Create Bedrock Agent with Data Extraction Schema
implementation.description: Define agent with knowledge base and action group for schema-guided extraction.
implementation.code:
import boto3
import json

bedrock_agent = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

agent_config = {
    'agentId': 'your-agent-id',
    'agentAliasId': 'your-alias-id',
    'sessionId': 'extraction-session-123',
    'inputText': 'Extract invoice data: {{document_content}}',
    'enableTrace': True
}

response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(**agent_config)

# Stream response
stream = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent_stream(**agent_config)
for event in stream:
    if 'completion' in event:
        chunk = event['completion']['chunk']['bytes'].decode()
        print(chunk, end='')

vexIntegration.title: Multi-Turn Session Consistency Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify field consistency across agent conversation turns.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

session_state = {}

async def verified_multi_turn_extraction(session_id: str, turn_input: str):
    global session_state
    
    response = bedrock_agent.invoke_agent(
        agentId='your-agent-id',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=turn_input
    )
    
    # Verify session consistency
    verified = vex.verify(
        response,
        session=session_state,
        assertions=[
            # Check amount consistency across turns
            lambda data: 'amount' not in session_state or 
                        abs(data['amount'] - session_state['amount']) < 0.01,
            # Ensure incremental completeness
            lambda data: len(data.keys()) >= len(session_state.keys())
        ]
    )
    
    session_state.update(verified)
    return verified
